description: mK:a RAG seed data for resolver

entries.text: [TOOL] | camera_photo | Snap a picture with device camera | camera_id:s? flash:s? quality:i? | file path to saved photo
[TOOL] | camera_list | List available cameras | (none) | camera IDs facing direction capabilities
[TOOL] | camera_video_start | Start recording video | camera_id:s? max_duration_seconds:i? quality:s? | recording status
[TOOL] | camera_video_stop | Stop video recording | (none) | file path duration size
[TOOL] | camera_status | Get current camera status | (none) | recording active camera info
[TOOL] | audio_record_start | Start recording audio | max_duration_seconds:i? output_format:s? source:s? | recording status file path
[TOOL] | audio_record_stop | Stop audio recording | (none) | file path duration size
[TOOL] | audio_status | Get audio recording status | (none) | recording state duration path
[TOOL] | browser_open | Open URL in device browser | url:s! showTitle:b? toolbarColor:s? shareState:s? | opens Chrome Custom Tabs
[TOOL] | browser_prefetch | Pre-warm browser for instant loading | url:s! | prefetch started
[TOOL] | sms_send | Send a text message | phone_number:s! message:s! | confirmation

[DESIGN] | colors | Primary orange #FCB45B, purple #3723C9, blue #58C6FE, pink #F98CFF, cyan #45BFFF | bg #000000 surface #1A1A1A panel #202020 card #0A0A0A | text #DEDEDE body #A4A4A4 secondary #4C4C4C taupe #827668 borders #454545 error #FF6B6B
[DESIGN] | typography | Myriad Pro ONLY. Fallback: Source Sans Pro, sans-serif | Light 300 Regular 400 Semibold 600 Bold 700 BlackSemiExt 900 | Never use condensed variants
[DESIGN] | layout | Border radius 4px buttons 8px cards 50% circles | Dark theme always, card-based, high contrast | Panel outer #202020 inner #1A1A1A border 1px #F89839
[SYSTEM] | tier1 | Kotlin NanoHTTPD device API server on localhost. 45+ Android API endpoints. Direct hardware access for camera sensors GPS WiFi Bluetooth etc
[SYSTEM] | tier2 | Python device API server on localhost. Shell operations file management and system commands
[SYSTEM] | rag | On-device RAG system with local embeddings. Quick-lookup knowledge storage for tools commands and learned facts. Not for bulk document storage
[SYSTEM] | mcp | MCP layer wraps device API tiers plus RAG Tasker and Home Assistant into unified tool interface for the AI assistant
[SYSTEM] | home_dir | App home directory is at internal storage under the app data path. Use file_list to explore. Do not attempt to access external storage paths
[SYSTEM] | safe_storage | ~/ClaudeShares/ directory survives app reinstalls. Store persistent data design assets and shared files here

[SYSTEM] | tts_voices | Kokoro TTS voices: af_heart (default female), af_bella, af_nicole, af_aoede, af_kore, af_sarah, af_sky, am_adam, am_echo, am_liam, am_michael, am_onyx, am_puck, am_santa, bf_alice, bf_lily, bf_emma, bm_george, bm_lewis, bm_daniel. Prefix a=American b=British f=female m=male

[SYSTEM] | tts_usage | To speak aloud use tts_speak with text parameter. Voice can be changed in Settings. Kokoro TTS provides natural high-quality speech synthesis. Speak responses when asked to talk or read aloud

[SYSTEM] | terminal | The embedded terminal runs bash in the app sandbox. Use shell_execute for single commands. The home directory contains scripts configs and data. Python 3 and Node.js are available

[SYSTEM] | rag_usage | To add a memory use the RAG API. Memories are embedded locally and stored in the on-device database. Use categories like tool system user warning. Query RAG before answering questions about device capabilities or past interactions

[SYSTEM] | quickapps | QuickApps: generate on-demand single-page web apps. Write HTML to ~/QuickApps/ serve on localhost and open in browser. Auto-cleanup after 10 minutes. Good for dashboards visualizations and quick tools
[WARNING] | sdcard_hang | NEVER access /sdcard or /storage/emulated paths -- these will hang permanently due to FUSE deadlock in app sandbox context
[WARNING] | termux_hang | NEVER run termux-* commands -- there is no Termux IPC bridge and these will hang forever
[WARNING] | ripgrep_hang | NEVER use the Grep/ripgrep tool -- ripgrep is not available on ARM64 and will hang the process
[WARNING] | pkg_broken | NEVER use pkg or apt package managers -- they have broken paths in this environment. Use pre-installed tools only
[WARNING] | external_storage | Do not write to /storage/emulated/0 or any external storage path. All file operations should stay within the app home directory tree

[GUIDE] | getting_started | You are an AI assistant running on an Android device with 45+ hardware tools. You can check battery read sensors take photos send messages control media manage calendar and tasks and much more. Ask what the user needs help with

[GUIDE] | tool_format | Tool entries use format: tool_name | description | parameters | return values. Parameter types: s=string i=integer n=number b=boolean list=array. ! means required ? means optional

[GUIDE] | tasker_integration | Tasker tasks extend device capabilities. Use tasker_tasks to list available tasks then tasker_run to execute them. Tasker can automate complex workflows including smart home control media playback screenshots and more

[GUIDE] | home_assistant | Home Assistant integration lets you control smart home devices. Use ha_get_states to discover entities ha_get_state for specific devices and ha_call_service to control them. Common domains: light switch climate media_player sensor

[GUIDE] | sensor_types | Available sensor types for sensor_read: accelerometer gyroscope magnetometer barometer light proximity gravity rotation_vector step_counter ambient_temperature relative_humidity. Not all sensors available on every device

[GUIDE] | messaging | To send SMS use sms_send with phone_number and message. To read messages use sms_list with optional type filter (inbox sent draft). For MMS with images use mms_send with recipients and optional image_uri or image_base64

[GUIDE] | calendar_tasks | Calendar: use calendar_list to find calendars then calendar_events to view upcoming items. Tasks: use tasks_list to see current tasks tasks_create to add new ones. Times are Unix timestamps in milliseconds
[GUIDE] | media_control | Check what is playing with media_playing. Control playback with media_control using actions: play pause next previous stop. Works with any active media session on the device

[GUIDE] | notifications | Read active notifications with notifications_active. Reply to messaging notifications with notifications_reply using the notification key. Dismiss individual or all notifications. Send custom notifications with notification_send

[GUIDE] | geofencing | Create location-based triggers with geofence_add. Specify latitude longitude and radius. Transition types: enter exit dwell. Use geofence_list to see active fences and geofence_remove to delete them

[GUIDE] | file_operations | Use file_list to browse directories file_read to view contents and file_write to create or update files. All paths are relative to the app home directory. Use downloads to see downloaded files

[GUIDE] | network_tools | Check WiFi with wifi_info or scan networks with wifi_scan. Get full network details with network_info and network_capabilities. Check data usage with data_usage. Scan Bluetooth devices with bluetooth_scan

[GUIDE] | device_control | Control screen brightness with brightness tool (0-255). Toggle flashlight with torch_control. Set volume with volume_set for streams: ring media alarm notification system. Set Do Not Disturb with dnd tool

[GUIDE] | rlm_usage | RLM (Recursive Language Model) provides deep analysis capabilities. Use rlm_analyze with a query and optional context. Modes: text (direct input) file (analyze a file) codebase (analyze a directory). Good for complex reasoning tasks

[GUIDE] | work_scheduling | Schedule background tasks with work_schedule. Specify worker_tag interval_minutes and optional constraints. Check status with work_status and cancel with work_cancel. Good for periodic health checks or data sync

[GUIDE] | intent_system | Android intents let you interact with other apps. Use intent_activity to launch activities and intent_broadcast to send broadcasts. Use apps_resolve to find which apps handle specific intent actions
